title: Meaty Egyptian Beetroot Stew
description: A hearty one-pot Egyptian dish featuring beetroot, toasted bread, and high-protein ingredients, with a rich winey flavor and chewy texture. This stew is perfect for a cold winter evening.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced beetroot and cook for 5 minutes, until slightly tender.
Add the red wine, lentils, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Add the toasted bread to the stew and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, until the bread is chewy and the stew is heated through.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
